heading: Residents of Retirement Home
label: 412 Residents of Retirement Home
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (e)(2), was in the original “this Act”, and was translated as meaning title XV of , , , known as the Armed Forces Retirement Home Act of 1991, which is classified principally to this chapter, to reflect the probable intent of Congress. children.heading: Persons eligible to be residents
children.label: a Persons eligible to be residents

children.text: Persons who were discharged or released from service in the Armed Forces after 20 or more years of active service.
Persons who are determined under rules prescribed by the Chief Operating Officer to be suffering from a service-connected disability incurred in the line of duty in the Armed Forces.

Persons who served in a war theater during a time of war declared by Congress or were eligible for hostile fire special pay under section 310 or 351 of title 37 and who are determined under rules prescribed by the Chief Operating Officer to be suffering from injuries, disease, or disability.

Persons who served in a women’s component of the Armed Forces before , and are determined under rules prescribed by the Chief Operating Officer to be eligible for admission because of compelling personal circumstances.June 12, 1948

children.label: A
children.text: eligible for care under ;section 1710 of title 38

children.label: B
children.text: enrolled in coverage under chapter 55 of title 10; or

children.label: C
children.text: enrolled in a qualified health plan (as defined in ) that is acceptable to the Chief Operating Officer.section 18021(a) of title 42

children.heading: Persons ineligible to be residents
children.label: b Persons ineligible to be residents
A
children.text: has been convicted of a felony; or

children.label: B
children.text: was discharged or released from service in the Armed Forces under other than honorable conditions.

children.label: A
children.text: the person has been evaluated by a qualified health professional selected by the Retirement Home;

children.label: B
children.text: the Retirement Home can accommodate the person’s condition; and

children.label: C
children.text: the person agrees to such conditions of residency as the Retirement Home may require.

children.heading: Acceptance
children.label: c Acceptance
children.text: To apply for acceptance as a resident of a facility of the Retirement Home, a person eligible to be a resident shall submit to the Administrator of that facility an application in such form and containing such information as the Chief Operating Officer may require.

children.heading: Priorities for acceptance
children.label: d Priorities for acceptance
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer shall establish a system of priorities for the acceptance of residents so that the most deserving applicants will be accepted whenever the number of eligible applicants is greater than the Retirement Home can accommodate.

children.label: A
children.text: is a covered beneficiary within the meaning of ;section 1072(5) of title 10

children.label: B
children.text: is not ineligible to become a resident as provided in subsection (b); and

children.label: C
children.text: submits an application for admittance in accordance with subsection (c).

children.heading: Treatment as resident
children.label: 2 Treatment as resident
children.text: A spouse admitted in accordance with paragraph (1) shall be a resident of the Retirement Home consistent with this chapter, except as the Chief Operating Officer may otherwise provide.
